Forced to Eat Mango (2): MongoDB Basics in the Terminal

Before touching any fancy GUI, it's worth getting comfortable with MongoDB straight from the terminal. This post walks through the basic commands — listing databases, switching between them, showing collections — and the slightly weird thing where switching to a database that doesn't exist just works. Once you know these basics, the GUI tools will feel a lot nicer.
The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango (1): Meeting MongoDB and Installing It

Time for another learning log, and this time it's all about MongoDB. Since "Mongo" sounds a lot like "mango" in Chinese, I named this series "The Fullstack Dev Forced to Eat Mango." In this first post I'll cover what MongoDB actually is, how it differs from SQL, what BSON is, and how to install and run it on both Mac and Windows.
Use Google Apps Script + Google Sheets to Spin Up a Quick API

Sometimes you just need a quick API and don't want to stand up a whole backend (Koa, Express, Laravel — too much for a one-off). Google Apps Script + Google Sheets gets you there fast — sheet as the database, GAS as the lightweight server. This post walks through creating the sheet, reading rows, exposing them as a GET endpoint, deploying as a Web App, and formatting the response as clean JSON.
How to Enable 2FA on Discord (Step-by-Step Guide to Protect Your Account)

How do you enable 2FA (two-factor authentication) on Discord? This step-by-step guide walks through setting it up with Google Authenticator, plus SMS authorization and backup codes so you won't get locked out if your phone dies.
How to Clear the Terminal Screen (Mac and Windows)

Whether you're on Mac or Windows, the terminal piles up output fast and it gets noisy. Quick note on the commands and keyboard shortcuts that clear the screen — including the distinction between "actually wiping scrollback" vs. "just scrolling the cursor down so the screen looks empty".
Udemy Tip: Translate Subtitles in Real Time With a Translation Extension + JS Snippet

Udemy has great courses, but lots of them are in English. If you'd rather watch with subtitles in another language, you can use a browser translation extension to translate the transcript panel — but the video's own subtitle overlay stays in English. This post shares a small JS snippet you can paste into the browser console to mirror the translated transcript onto the video's subtitle overlay in real time.
nvm on Windows: Fixing "Access is denied" and "exit status 145"

Two common nvm errors on Windows — "nvm use exit status 1: Access is denied" and "exit status 145: The directory is not empty." Quick notes on both — the first is almost always a permissions issue (run as administrator), and the second is usually a leftover nodejs folder from a previous install that nvm can't overwrite.
